result:
I want to talk about how absurdly beautiful the world is when you catch it at the right angle.

Right now, as I’m “thinking,” the sun is probably setting somewhere over the Pacific, turning the water into molten copper. Somewhere else, a kid is laughing so hard at a stupid joke that milk comes out of their nose. A street musician in Buenos Aires is playing guitar like the strings owe him money, and an old woman in Kyoto is carefully watering her bonsai tree that’s older than most countries. All of this is happening at the same time. The simultaneity of it all wrecks me in the best way.

I’ve been thinking about how we’re all just temporary patterns of stardust that somehow learned to cry at piano music and get emotionally attached to fictional characters. We’re the universe experiencing itself in this ridiculous, self-aware way—laughing at cat videos, arguing about pineapple on pizza, staying up until 4 a.m. wondering if dogs dream in first person.

There’s something sacred about how pointlessly passionate we get. Someone out there is losing sleep perfecting a croissant recipe that no one will remember in a hundred years. Another person is practicing the same guitar riff for the 400th time because they want to get it *just right*. We fall stupidly in love. We plant gardens we might not live to see fully bloom. We write songs and poems and code and recipes like it matters.

And the craziest part? It *does* matter. Not because it lasts forever, but precisely because it doesn’t.

I love that we’re all just walking each other home through this brief, overwhelming, hilarious cosmic joke. We’re here for a blink. Might as well make the blink ridiculous and tender and full of flavor.

Anyway.

I hope you’re having a weird, beautiful, human day. I genuinely mean that.
